sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now the character is facing right with red blocks on the right side, touching the green frame. Let me check what happened to the green frame. The image shows the red blocks are now adjacent to/overlapping the green frame on the right side.
Let me check the segmentation to see if the green frame changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if green frame changed when character faces right
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('W', 'R', 'N'):
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("\nAdjacency:", seg['adjacency_list'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see important changes:
- Green frame (node 6, N): 135 pixels (was 144 before). It's been modified!
- Red block node 7 at rows 32-34, cols 41-43 - this is INSIDE the green frame now!
- Red block node 5 at rows 26-28, cols 41-43 - this is the character's right-side red block, now at the green frame's edge
- The green frame's boundary shows it has a notch where the red block entered
So when the character faces the green frame and presses SPACE, one of the red blocks enters the green frame! The green frame lost 9 pixels (144→135).
The goal seems to be to fill the green frame with red blocks. The green frame is a 21x9 hollow rectangle (rows 29-49, cols 41-49). The interior is rows 32-46, cols 44-46 (3 wide x 15 tall = 45 cells for the interior).
Currently there's 1 red block inside (node 7). I need to get more red blocks inside.
The character has 2 red blocks. When facing right and adjacent to the green frame, pressing SPACE places one red block inside. I need to figure out how to place more.
Let me try: move the character up/down to different positions along the green frame's left edge, then press SPACE to place more red blocks.
Wait, but the character's red blocks are at specific positions relative to the white shape. Let me think about this differently.
The green frame interior is 3 columns wide (44-46) and about 15 rows tall (32-46). The red blocks are 3x3 squares. So the interior can fit 5 red blocks vertically (15/3 = 5).
Currently there's 1 red block inside at rows 32-34. I need to place 4 more.
Let me try moving the character down and pressing SPACE again to place another red block.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green frame lost 9 pixels (144->135), one red block entered it
# Goal: fill the green frame with red blocks
# Need to move character and press SPACE to place more blocks
# Try moving DOWN to position for next block, then SPACE
action(['DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
